que: What is a primary goal of modularization in Android projects?
options: To split an app into smaller maintainable modules
To store all code in one file

que: Which module usually acts as the final Android application entry point?
What is a common benefit of modularization?
Which type of module is a good place for reusable Compose components?
Why should circular dependencies between modules be avoided?
options: They improve compile speed too much
They make architecture harder to maintain and build

que: What is a good reason to create feature modules?
options: To isolate screens or user flows

que: What is a common modularization mistake?
options: Creating too many modules without purpose

que: Where should app-specific wiring usually stay?
How can modules communicate more safely?
What Compose-related benefit comes from modularization?
options: Reusable composables can be placed in shared UI modules

que: What should happen before adding many modules?

options: It organizes code into well-defined modules with explicit responsibilities
It means every class becomes a module
It is only for very large desktop apps
